inputs.userGoal: Provision an event-driven data processing pipeline using Azure Container Apps with Dapr Pub/Sub integration, connected to Azure Event Grid as the event source, outputting processed results to Cosmos DB.

resources.name: log-datapipeline-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Required by Container Apps Environment for centralized log collection and diagnostics. PerGB2018 is the recommended default SKU for new deployments.
alternativesConsidered: Azure Monitor destination (less queryable)
No logging (not recommended for production)
reasoning.tradeoffs: Pay-per-GB costs scale with log volume; 30-day retention balances cost and debuggability.

resources.dependencyReasoning: No upstream dependencies; foundational observability resource.

resources.name: evgt-datapipeline-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Event Grid provides a fully managed event routing service with low latency and high throughput. CloudEvents v1.0 schema is used for interoperability with Dapr. Basic SKU is sufficient without private endpoint requirements.
alternativesConsidered: Event Hubs (better for high-volume streaming, overkill for event routing)
Service Bus topic as sole ingestion point (less event-routing flexibility)
reasoning.tradeoffs: Basic SKU does not support private endpoints; upgrade to Premium if VNet-only access is needed.

resources.dependencyReasoning: No upstream dependencies; acts as the entry point for external events.

resources.name: sbns-datapipeline-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Service Bus Standard is the Dapr pub/sub broker backing the Container App subscription. Standard tier supports topics (required for pub/sub pattern). Event Grid routes events to a Service Bus topic, and Dapr subscribes from it.
alternativesConsidered: Redis as Dapr pub/sub broker (less durable, no dead-lettering)
Premium Service Bus (overkill without VNet/zone requirements)
reasoning.tradeoffs: Standard tier uses shared capacity (no guaranteed throughput); upgrade to Premium for dedicated resources and VNet support.

resources.dependencyReasoning: No upstream dependencies; standalone messaging namespace.

resources.name: sbt-datapipeline-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Service Bus topic acts as the Dapr pub/sub channel. Event Grid delivers events here via an event subscription, and the Container App consumes via Dapr subscriber.
alternativesConsidered: Service Bus queue (no fan-out to multiple subscribers)
reasoning.tradeoffs: Topics add slight overhead vs queues but enable multiple subscribers for future expansion.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Topic is a child resource of the Service Bus namespace.

resources.name: sbts-datapipeline-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: The Dapr pub/sub component requires a Service Bus subscription on the topic to deliver messages to the Container App consumer.
alternativesConsidered: Multiple subscriptions for fan-out (not needed for single consumer)
reasoning.tradeoffs: Single subscription limits to one consumer group; add more subscriptions if multiple processors are needed.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Subscription is a child resource of the Service Bus topic.

resources.name: evgt-datapipeline-prod-001/evgs-to-servicebus

reasoning.whyChosen: Routes events from Event Grid topic to the Service Bus topic, bridging the Event Grid ingress with the Dapr pub/sub consumer. CloudEvents schema ensures Dapr compatibility.
alternativesConsidered: Webhook directly to Container App (tighter coupling, no dead-letter support)
Event Hub destination (unnecessary for this throughput)
reasoning.tradeoffs: Adds a hop (Event Grid → Service Bus → Container App) but gains durable delivery, retry, and dead-lettering.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Child of Event Grid topic; targets Service Bus topic as delivery endpoint.

resources.name: cosmos-datapipeline-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Cosmos DB NoSQL API is ideal for storing semi-structured event processing results with low-latency reads/writes. Serverless mode eliminates idle costs for intermittent event-driven workloads. Session consistency balances performance and correctness.
alternativesConsidered: Azure SQL Database (better for relational data, overkill for JSON documents)
Azure Table Storage (lower cost but limited querying capabilities)
Provisioned throughput Cosmos DB (better for sustained high-throughput)
reasoning.tradeoffs: Serverless is single-region only with no multi-region writes. Upgrade to provisioned throughput if sustained >1M RU/s is needed.

resources.dependencyReasoning: No upstream dependencies; standalone data store.

resources.name: cosmos-datapipeline-prod-001/pipeline-db

reasoning.whyChosen: SQL database container to hold the processed event results. Required child resource of the Cosmos DB account.
alternativesConsidered: MongoDB API database (less native to Azure)
reasoning.tradeoffs: None significant; NoSQL API is the default and most integrated option.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Child resource of Cosmos DB account.

resources.name: cosmos-datapipeline-prod-001/pipeline-db/processed-events

reasoning.whyChosen: Container to store processed event results. Partitioned by eventType for balanced distribution across event categories.
alternativesConsidered: Partition by event source ID (better if single source dominates)
reasoning.tradeoffs: Partition key choice affects query performance and distribution; can be changed by creating a new container.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Child resource of the SQL database.

resources.name: cae-datapipeline-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Container Apps Environment provides the managed hosting platform with built-in Dapr support. Consumption workload profile gives serverless scaling with zero idle cost, matching the event-driven workload pattern.
alternativesConsidered: Dedicated workload profiles (better for sustained high load)
AKS cluster (full control but higher operational overhead)
reasoning.tradeoffs: Consumption profile has limited CPU/memory per instance (max 4 vCPU/8 GiB). Upgrade to dedicated profiles for larger workloads.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Log Analytics workspace must exist before the Container Apps Environment so log configuration can reference its customerId and sharedKey.

resources.name: cae-datapipeline-prod-001/dapr-pubsub-servicebus

reasoning.whyChosen: Dapr pub/sub component backed by Azure Service Bus enables the Container App to subscribe to events via the Dapr programming model, decoupling application code from messaging infrastructure.
alternativesConsidered: Dapr pub/sub with Redis (less durable, no dead-letter support)
Direct Service Bus SDK integration (tighter coupling)
reasoning.tradeoffs: Dapr adds a sidecar per container instance but provides portable pub/sub abstraction and built-in retry/dead-letter handling.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Dapr component is a child of the Container Apps Environment and requires the Service Bus namespace connection string.

references.title: Dapr pub/sub with Azure Service Bus

resources.name: ca-eventprocessor-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Container App with Dapr enabled subscribes to Service Bus via Dapr pub/sub, processes events, and writes results to Cosmos DB. KEDA-based scaling on Service Bus message count ensures scale-to-zero when idle and auto-scale under load. 0.5 vCPU / 1 GiB is appropriate for moderate event processing.
alternativesConsidered: Azure Functions with Service Bus trigger (simpler but less flexible container packaging)
AKS pod with Dapr (full control but higher operational cost)
reasoning.tradeoffs: Scale-to-zero saves cost but introduces cold-start latency (~1–2s). Set minReplicas to 1 if latency-sensitive.

resources.dependencyReasoning: Container App requires the environment, the Dapr component for pub/sub, and Cosmos DB for output.

overallReasoning.summary: This architecture implements an event-driven data processing pipeline using serverless Azure services. Events enter via an Event Grid topic, are routed to a Service Bus topic (enabling durable Dapr pub/sub), consumed by a Container App with Dapr, and results are persisted to Cosmos DB Serverless. All compute uses Consumption/Serverless tiers for cost-efficient scale-to-zero behavior.
overallReasoning.tradeoffs: The Event Grid → Service Bus → Dapr → Container App chain adds one hop vs direct webhook delivery, but gains durable messaging, dead-letter support, and Dapr abstraction. Serverless tiers across the stack optimize for intermittent workloads but may need upgrading for sustained high-throughput scenarios. No VNet integration in this default plan — add if network isolation is required.

plan.validation: All resources deploy to eastus. Container Apps Environment references Log Analytics workspace. Container App references the environment ID. Dapr pub/sub component is scoped to the event processor app and references the Service Bus namespace. Event Grid subscription routes to the Service Bus topic. Cosmos DB is serverless single-region with Session consistency. Service Bus Standard supports topics. All naming follows CAF conventions and length/character constraints.

references.title: Azure Event Grid to Service Bus integration
